data.title: Sweetgreen sees green on upbeat outlook
data.description:
Shares of Sweetgreen (NYSE:SG) are soaring in Friday’s trade after the fast-casual restaurant chain cut its Q2 loss by nearly half, saw same-store sales jump 9%, and raised its FY24 guidance for a second time this year, launching shares as much as 32% higher.

The introduction of steak dishes seems to have connected with customers as Q2 revenues increased 21% to $184.6M, driving down the company’s loss to just $0.13 per share compared to a loss of $0.24 in the same quarter last year. Meanwhile, same store sales were up 9% year-over-year, coinciding with the introduction of meat to the predominantly all-salad menu, compared to a more modest increase of 3% in the same quarter last year. The solid results, and the addition of new restaurants led the company to increase its FY24 guidance for a second time above Wall Street’s expectations. Guidance that UBS believes could be conservative.

“Second quarter results highlighted strong [same store sales] and traffic, solid margin expansion, and encouraging progress on Infinite Kitchen plans which support a compelling long-term growth outlook,” UBS analyst Dennis Geiger said. He maintained his Buy rating on Sweetgreen and lifted the target price by 19% to $37, a 40% premium to Thursday’s closing price.

Morgan Stanley remains neutral on Sweetgreen (SG) with an Equal Weight rating, but gave the price target a little boost to $27 as the firm was more cautious in its outlook given slowing consumer trends and outlook for the total addressable market, or TAM.

“There are still longer-term debates on TAM to be had and [how] this affects valuation, but the narrative is building on how both automation and menu broadening can expand the opportunity for [Sweetgreen],” Morgan Stanley’s Brian Harbour says.

But caution is warranted, CFO Mitch Reback said on the company’s earnings call. “We feel like we do not control the outside world, and we kind of read the same stuff in the same papers everybody else reads and reports on, and I think we have a degree of cautiousness around the external environment,” Reback said.

Morgan Stanley cites the challenging consumer environment along with any lingering cost inflation as a potential downside to the stock’s rating and price target, but success in automation and increasing margins should insulate the company from sustained shocks from changes in consumer spending.

data.title: Cisco to cut 'thousands' more in second round of layoffs this year: report
data.description:
• Cisco Systems (NASDAQ:CSCO) is set to let go "thousands" of employees later this year in what would mark the networking giant's second round of employee cutbacks this year, Reuters reported.
  • The number of people impacted by the layoffs could be similar or "slightly higher" than the 4,000 employees, or roughly 5% of the company's workforce, that were let go in February, the news outlet added, citing people familiar with the matter.
  • The cuts could be announced in conjunction with Cisco's fiscal fourth-quarter results, which are slated to be released after the close of trading on Wednesday. A consensus of analysts expect Cisco to earn $0.85 per share on $13.54B in revenue for the quarter.
  • Cisco did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.
  • Shares of the San Jose, Calif-based Cisco were down 0.7% in early trading on Friday.

data.title: Compania Cervecerias Unidas S.A. reports Q2 results
data.description:
• Compania Cervecerias Unidas S.A. press release (NYSE:CCU): Q2 Net sales were down 8.6%
  • Gross profit decreased 15.8%
  • EBITDA reached CLP 38,722 million3. Excluding the non-recurring gain from the sale of a portion of land in Chile, EBITDA totalized CLP 10,053 million, a 78.7% decrease.
  • Net income reached a gain of CLP 5,040 million3. Excluding the non-recurring gain from the sale of a portion of land in Chile, Net income totalized a loss of CLP 15,888 million, versus a loss of CLP 3,943 million last year.
  • Earnings per share reached CLP 13.6 per share

data.title: Millicom says Atlas Luxco's latest offer still undervalues company
data.description:
Millicom (NASDAQ:TIGO) said its independent committee believes that Atlas Luxco's latest offer "significantly" undervalues the Latin American phone carrier.

Millicom (TIGO) was responding to an increased $4.4 billion or $25.75 a share bid, up from $24, from billionaire Xavier Niel Atlas Luxco last week.

The committee recommends that shareholders do not accept the offer, according to a statement earlier on Friday.

The revised offer prices "continues to be well below trading multiples for comparable listed companies," according to the statement.

Millicom's independent board in late June rejected the Atlas bid.

data.title: Paramount Global upgraded at Wells Fargo ahead of its deal closing with Skydance
data.description:
Paramount Global (NASDAQ:PARA) was upgraded to "equal weight" from a previous investment rating of "underweight" at Wells Fargo as it inches near closing its merger with Skydance. They say the deal structure limits fundamental upside/downside for the company's shares.

"We see PARA shares as rangebound pending the Skdyance merger, which is set to close in 1H'25. Recall Skydance will purchase up to 50% of B shares at $15/sh, so post-tender offer PARA is currently implied pro forma at ~$7/sh. This value is subject to further details from the deal filings, more info on the improvement plan from new management, ongoing industry trends, etc. Incoming management certainly thinks it can be higher and will be working accordingly," Wells analysts wrote in an August 9 analysis.

Wells noted that PARA and WBD "are more alike than different" but say 5.5-6x is the right EV/EBITDA multiple for PARA until things prove otherwise, after seeing WBD's disappointing deal aftermath, which was valued at a higher multiple by the research firm.

The research firm now expects the company's 2024 EPS to be $1.93 on revenue of $30.13B. It expects FCF of $338.7M and AOIBDA of $3.2B. PT for PARA was hiked by a dollar to $11, an upside of nearly 8%.

PARA is -31% while the S&P 500 index is +11.5% YTD.

data.title: Foxconn hires 50,000 Chinese workers to boost production for iPhone 16: report
data.description:
• Foxconn (OTCPK:FXCOF) recently hired 50,000 new employees for its factory in Zhengzhou, China, to boost production for Apple's upcoming iPhone 16, according to a report by Business Korea.
  • Foxconn's Zhengzhou factory is the world's most prolific production base for the iPhone, the report said.
  • Taiwan-based Foxconn builds approximately two out of every three iPhones, in addition to other devices such as the Google (GOOG)(GOOGL) Pixel and Sony (SONY) PlayStation, according to Bloomberg.
  • Apple looks to ship millions more iPhones than previously expected when the product is launched sometime next month. Wedbush now predicts initial iPhone 16 shipments will approach 90M, versus earlier estimates ranging from 80M to 84M units.
  • Despite geopolitical concerns over China, Apple remains committed to doing business there. Last month, Apple's Chief Operating Officer Jeff Williams visited political leaders in Shenzhen to reinforce the importance of the PRC for its products.
